∿ Nourishing Raw Cacao Drink

One of my favourite evening rituals. Warm, grounding, and genuinely nourishing.

Recipe
  • 1 mug milk of choice (raw goat, raw cow, coconut, homemade hemp or almond milk)
  • Warm gently on the stove
  • 1–2 tsp organic raw cacao (low heavy metal tested if possible)
  • Pinch of Ceylon cinnamon
  • 1 tsp melted ghee and or coconut oil
  • Raw honey or maple syrup to taste

If using nuts or seeds for homemade milk, soak overnight and rinse well before blending.

∿ Simple Homemade Chocolate
Ingredients
  • Melted coconut oil
  • Melted cacao butter
  • 100% raw organic cacao powder
  • Melted ghee
  • Raw honey
  • Vanilla cinnamon infused maple syrup (Vermont is the best)
  • Pinch of sea salt
  • Vanilla extract
Optional Toppings
  • Chopped nuts (soaked)
  • Coconut flakes
  • Cinnamon
  • Flaky salt
  • Dried berries
∿ Natural Home Spray Recipe
What You Need
  • Amber or clear glass spray bottle
  • Distilled water
  • A few drops of witch hazel or vodka (helps the oil mix)
  • 15–20 drops essential oil of choice
∿ Natural Dish Soap Recipe
Ingredients
  • Liquid castile soap
  • Warm distilled water
  • Essential oil of choice
  • Vegetable glycerin
Add-Ons for Oily and Greasy Dishes
  • White vinegar
  • Washing soda or baking soda
  • Salt to thicken
  • Lemon or lemon juice
∿ Natural Candles
What to Look For
  • 100% pure beeswax with no additives or synthetic fragrance. They purify the air, release negative ions, and burn cleanly without toxins
Why It Matters
  • Conventional candles are made from paraffin (a petroleum byproduct) and release toxic chemicals when burned
  • Synthetic fragrance in candles is one of the most overlooked sources of indoor air pollution, it disrupts your hormones and can cause allergies, sinus issues, skin issues, headaches, and even fertility issues
∿ Top 10 Air-Purifying Plants for Hormone Health

The most impactful for hormone health are peace lily, snake plant, and English ivy. They target the chemicals most commonly linked to endocrine disruption.

PlantWhat It Does
Peace LilyRemoves benzene, formaldehyde, trichloroethylene, ammonia. Great for bedrooms, thrives in low light.
Snake PlantConverts CO2 to oxygen at night. Removes formaldehyde, benzene, xylene. Nearly impossible to kill.
Spider PlantRemoves formaldehyde and xylene. Safe for pets. Easy to grow.
English IvyHighly effective at reducing airborne mold and fecal particles. Great for bathrooms.
Aloe VeraReleases oxygen at night, removes formaldehyde and benzene. Also signals poor air quality through brown spots. Great for skin and gut healing.
Boston FernOne of the best for removing formaldehyde. Acts as a natural humidifier.
Rubber PlantExcellent at removing formaldehyde. Very low maintenance.
Areca PalmOne of the best natural humidifiers. Filters xylene and toluene.
ChrysanthemumRemoves benzene, formaldehyde, ammonia. Needs bright light.
PothosRemoves benzene, formaldehyde, xylene, toluene. Extremely hard to kill. Perfect for beginners.
∿ Air-Purifying Flowers
FlowerWhat It Does
LavenderReduces airborne bacteria and mold. The scent alone measurably lowers cortisol.
JasminePurifies air and reduces anxiety. Improves sleep quality which directly supports hormones.
GeraniumRemoves airborne bacteria and repels insects naturally without toxic chemicals.
Gerbera DaisyReleases oxygen at night and filters trichloroethylene and benzene.
RosesAbsorb mold spores and some VOCs. Rose essential oil has phytoestrogenic properties.
CalendulaAnti-inflammatory, antifungal, edible petals. Particularly supportive for women's cycles.
"Let's stop thinking that our cycles are trying to make our lives harder, because they are not. They are one of the most honest, intelligent and beautiful systems our body has."
With love and belief in your healing 🌺

It is not intended to diagnose, treat, cure, or prevent any condition or disease. I am not a medical doctor or licensed healthcare professional. Always consult with a qualified healthcare provider before making changes to your diet, lifestyle, or health routine, especially if you have an existing medical condition.
